feat: escalation health metrics, overflow improvements, open-source prep

## sinain-core
- Escalator: track totalTimeouts, totalDirectResponses, totalSpawnResponses,
  avgResponseMs (EMA α=0.2), consecutiveTimeouts, lastTimeoutTs
- Warn on 3+ consecutive timeouts or >30% timeout rate at 10-sample intervals
- getHealthPayload: surface computed warnings[] array for high_timeout_rate,
  consecutive_timeouts, stale_responses, no_direct_responses, slow_responses

## sinain-hud-plugin
- Detect "extra usage is required" as overflow trigger (was missed by old regex)
- Duration-gated overflow reset: >3min failed run + overflow error = stuck retry loop;
  truncate transcript immediately and send Telegram alert
- Inject HEARTBEAT enforcement and SITUATION.md bootstrap into agent context
  (replaces fork-only system-prompt.ts / workspace.ts logic)

## sinain-koog
- signals schema: items are now objects {description, priority} instead of strings
- recommendedAction.task: allow null (for skip actions)
- insight_synthesizer: `skip` no longer required
- Tests: update fixtures and add null-task + skip-less insight test cases

## sinain-wearable-hud / sinain-mobile / sinain-mobile-custom
- Remove responseTarget:"self" from agent RPC (not supported in current gateway)
- Default WS URL changed from hardcoded server to localhost:18789

## Docs & open-source prep
- Anonymize all private IPs/SSH keys across docs (use placeholders)
- README: add sense_client Quick Start step; link anthillnet/openclaw fork
- README: link new ESCALATION-HEALTH.md
- Add docs/ESCALATION-HEALTH.md: health warnings table, failure mode runbooks,
  config knobs, proactive session hygiene explanation
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md and LICENSE (MIT)
- Add deploy/ (Dockerfile.openclaw, docker-compose.openclaw.yml)
- Add sense_client/README.md with architecture diagram
- .gitignore: exclude photo_*.jpg at repo root

Persist web server via launchd KeepAlive

The server previously required manual startup and died whenever the
terminal was closed or the machine rebooted.

Changes:

- com.you.prospecting-web.plist: LaunchAgent with RunAtLoad + KeepAlive
  (starts on login, auto-restarts on any exit)
- server.py: add --no-reload flag; launchd uses it to run a single
  stable process instead of uvicorn's file-watching reloader
- README: Web Server section with management commands

commit.message:
Fix auto-buff activation + change entropy to diminishing returns

- Fixed activateManaBuff: removed setTimeout wrapper that prevented
  buffs from being added reliably (especially Fate Weaver)
- Synced activeManaBuffsRef during render instead of async useEffect
- Changed entropy: buffs no longer decay faster, instead each stack
  beyond 2nd gives half the power (3rd = 50%, 4th = 25%, etc.)
- Applied diminishing returns to all buff types including Fate Weaver

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: adicionar logica incremental aos agentes Copy e Satisfacao

- Ambos agentes: _get_previous_run(), _build_snapshot(), _get_new_video_ids()
- save_analysis: salva run_number + snapshot de videos analisados
- run_analysis: detecta videos novos, pula LLM se zero novos
- Retrocompativel: runs existentes (NULL) = todos NEW no proximo run
- Prompts LLM nao alterados (sera feito separadamente)

Compute office_name from hardcoded office code→name mapping

GMT→GMT, LAG-NG→LAG Nigeria, ABJ-NG→ABJ Nigeria, SA→South Africa,

for any unmapped codes. Applied to both incremental and full sync.

commit.message:
Add generic booking system (services, slots, reservations)

New bookings Blueprint with full user-facing and admin flows:

Models (models.py):
- BookableService — admin-managed bookable item (name, description, active flag)
- TimeSlot — date/start/end/capacity slot attached to a service
- Booking — user reservation (confirmed/cancelled, optional notes)

Routes (bookings/routes.py):
- GET  /bookings                     — search/browse active services
- GET  /bookings/<id>                — service detail + upcoming slots
- POST /bookings/<slot_id>/book      — create booking (capacity + dupe guard)
- GET  /bookings/confirm/<id>        — confirmation page
- GET  /bookings/my                  — user's own bookings
- POST /bookings/<id>/cancel         — cancel a booking
- GET  /admin/services               — admin: list all services + slots
- GET/POST /admin/services/new       — admin: create service
- GET/POST /admin/services/<id>/edit — admin: edit service
- POST /admin/services/<id>/delete   — admin: delete (blocked if has slots)
- GET/POST /admin/services/<id>/slots/new — admin: add time slot
- POST /admin/slots/<id>/delete      — admin: delete slot (blocked if booked)
- GET  /admin/bookings               — admin: read-only view of all bookings

Double-booking prevention: confirmed booking count checked against
slot.capacity before allowing a new booking. Same user cannot book
the same slot twice while confirmed.

NOTE: Delete app.db and run python seed.py before testing —
new tables require a fresh database.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix token embedding buffer placement for ROCm/HIP

Enable token embeddings to use GPU buffers on ROCm/HIP by recognizing
HIP0, HIP_Host, ROCm0, and ROCm_Host buffer names in addition to CUDA
buffer names. This fixes the fallback to CPU for q4_K quantized embeddings.

Expected performance impact: +5-10% throughput by eliminating PCI-E
roundtrips for every token embedding lookup.

Modified: src/llama-model.cpp (lines 3009-3016)
- Added pattern matching for HIP/ROCm buffer names
- Updated debug logging to reflect all buffer types

commit.message:
Fix get_stimulus_pdf in EncodingModel: pass Cholesky of omega, not omega itself

The base EncodingModel.get_stimulus_pdf was passing the raw covariance
matrix omega directly to _likelihood, where it is used as scale_tril
(the Cholesky factor) for MultivariateNormalTriL/MultivariateStudentT.
This caused tril(omega) @ tril(omega)^T to be used as the effective
covariance instead of omega, corrupting off-diagonal structure and
making the full WWT noise model perform much worse than spherical noise.

Mirrors the same fix already applied to EncodingRegressionModel (eefc6e0).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix node-exporter on WSL2: remove root mount and filesystem collector

the issue. CPU, memory, network, and load metrics still work via
/proc and /sys.

commit.message:
Hyperbolic transformer layers and refactoring (#26)

* add positional encoding and residual connection

* add hyperboloid RMSNorm

* remove functional calls

* Fix: Optimizer manifold metadata — store class instances instead of string registry

- Replace manifold_type: str → manifold: Manifold in parameter metadata
- Remove string-based registry (_MANIFOLD_REGISTRY, register_manifold, get_manifold_module)
- Optimizers now call public class methods (egrad2rgrad, expmap, retraction, ptransp, tangent_inner)
  instead of private module functions (_egrad2rgrad, _expmap, etc.)
- Store manifold instance directly in _var_metadata, so _cast() dtype control is applied during optimization
- Update NN layers (poincare_linear, poincare_regression) to pass manifold=self.manifold
- Update optim/__init__.py to remove registry imports and exports
- Update tests to use new API (manifold instances instead of string type)

All 1683 tests pass, 72 skipped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

S4: parallelize migration UPDATEs to overwhelm single-threaded replay

Single UPDATE session generates WAL at ~80 MB/s — replay at 500 MB/s
keeps up easily on fast HW. Fix: split into 4 concurrent UPDATE sessions
(each 500K rows), combined WAL rate ~320 MB/s approaches replay capacity.
Also increase probe from 60s to 90s for better overlap coverage.

fix: resolve email verification reload loop

Add separate emailVerified state to AuthProvider that updates via
setEmailVerified, allowing React to re-render when refreshEmailVerified()
is called after firebaseUser.reload(). Previously, mutating the Firebase
User object in-place didn't trigger re-renders, causing an infinite
redirect loop between /dashboard and /verify-email.

Rate limiting by IP remains in place.
